title: OCSP Responder and CRL Repository Reachability and Latency Monitoring

compliance.assurance_rationale: IA-5(2) PKI-based authentication — OCSP/CRL revocation enforcement.
A.8.24 cryptography — revocation checks part of crypto lifecycle.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Block OCSP responder URL via firewall. ISE emits 35102. UC-17.1.70 fires.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Certificate* category — codes `35100` (OCSP request sent), `35101` (OCSP responder reachable), `35102` (OCSP responder unreachable), `35103` (OCSP response latency), `35200` (CRL download successful), `35201` (CRL download failed). Field: `ocsp_responder_url`, `crl_repository_url`, `latency_ms`, `failure_reason`.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (35102, 35201, 35103)
| stats count by ocsp_responder_url, crl_repository_url, MessageCode, failure_reason
| where (MessageCode=="35102" AND count > 5) OR (MessageCode=="35201" AND count > 1) OR (MessageCode=="35103" AND count > 0)
description: Tracks OCSP responder availability and latency, and CRL repository download success/failure. Alerts when OCSP becomes unreachable, OCSP latency exceeds 2 seconds, or CRL downloads fail.
value: OCSP/CRL failures cause certificate revocation checks to fall back to a pre-configured behaviour (often 'allow' or 'use cached'). Either silently weakens authentication or causes mass auth failures depending on configuration. Visibility prevents both.
visualization: OCSP responder availability heatmap; CRL freshness by repository; latency distribution.
knownFalsePositives:
(a) Brief OCSP responder maintenance windows.
(b) Network blips affecting external OCSP responders.
grandmaExplanation: When a certificate is presented, ISE asks the issuing authority 'is this still valid?' over OCSP/CRL. If those services are down, ISE may silently approve or block everyone. We watch their health.
